title: Using `--sysctl` with `podman run` as root, but with a UID map set, fails. [Trying to set `net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf=1`]
body:
### Issue Description

Using `--sysctl` with `podman run` as root, but with a UID map set, fails when run as below:

```
root@control ~ [127]# podman run -d --name test --rm --network=podnettest --uidmap 0:1000:1 --uidmap 1:10000:65536 --sysctl net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf=1 quay.io/libpod/banner
Error: crun: open `/proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/eth0/autoconf`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Set up test veth link for nice clean environment. Substitute your ~main NIC or bridge~ for br0 below.

    root@control ~# ip link add dev vethtest0 type veth peer name vethtest1
    root@control ~# ip link set vethtest0 master br0
    root@control ~# ip link set vethtest0 up
    root@control ~# ip link set vethtest1 up

[Edit: realized this only works for me because I already have a bridge. The network topography doesn’t much matter for this report though, the important thing is to get an ipvlan (or macvlan) network set up below]

Create podman ipvlan network for testing.

    root@control ~# podman network create -d ipvlan --subnet 192.168.25.0/24 --ip-range 192.168.25.15/31 --gateway 192.168.25.1 podnettest


Test:

    root@control ~ [127]# podman run -d --name test --rm --network=podnettest --uidmap 0:1000:1 --uidmap 1:10000:65536 --sysctl net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf=1 quay.io/libpod/banner
    Error: crun: open `/proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/eth0/autoconf`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found


### Describe the results you received

Received error as above.

### Describe the results you expected

Expected sysctl to be set (as it is without using the UID map).

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information





Set up test veth link for nice clean environment. Substitute your main NIC or bridge for br0 below.

    root@control ~# ip link add dev vethtest0 type veth peer name vethtest1
    root@control ~# ip link set vethtest0 master br0
    root@control ~# ip link set vethtest0 up
    root@control ~# ip link set vethtest1 up


Create podman ipvlan network for testing.

    root@control ~# podman network create -d ipvlan --subnet 192.168.25.0/24 --ip-range 192.168.25.15/31 --gateway 192.168.25.1 podnettest



Default state: no autoconf IPv6:

    root@control ~# podman run -d --name test --rm --network=podnettest  quay.io/libpod/banner
    f13a96b7246067c8500592fe7b92af95a1279526f2dd1706e61bfacccde6cfe8
    root@control ~# podman exec -it test sysctl net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf
    net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf = 0
    root@control ~# podman exec -it test ip address
    1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
        link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
        inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
        inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    2: eth0@if3: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
        link/ether 1e:63:5b:4b:54:5a brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
        inet 192.168.25.15/24 brd 192.168.25.255 scope global eth0
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
        inet6 fe80::1e63:5b00:14b:545a/64 scope link 
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    root@control ~# podman stop test
    test


Okay, let's try with the sysctl setting - autoconf address is assigned!

    root@control ~# podman run -d --name test --rm --network=podnettest --sysctl net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf=1 quay.io/libpod/banner
    a8851d4f40fa1d04b8bbd27b32175acf524f08e98805675db25fa3c22f3dffe9
    root@control ~# podman exec -it test sysctl net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf
    net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf = 1
    root@control ~# podman exec -it test ip address
    1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
        link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
        inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
        inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    2: eth0@if3: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
        link/ether 1e:63:5b:4b:54:5a brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
        inet 192.168.25.15/24 brd 192.168.25.255 scope global eth0
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
        inet6 [--REDACTED IPv6 ADDRESS--]/64 scope global dynamic flags 100 
           valid_lft 318771sec preferred_lft 318771sec
        inet6 fe80::1e63:5b00:14b:545a/64 scope link 
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    root@control ~# podman stop test
    test


Now, with UID mapping and no sysctl - no IPv6 autoconf (as expected)

    root@control ~# podman run -d --name test --rm --network=podnettest --uidmap 0:1000:1 --uidmap 1:10000:65536 quay.io/libpod/banner
    bbea6af0b79c88aedf3e2cbb04f507d6097a75ae30b507a4c063325b228734b7
    root@control ~# podman exec -it test sysctl net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf
    net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf = 0
    root@control ~# podman exec -it test ip address
    1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
        link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
        inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
        inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    2: eth0@if3: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
        link/ether 1e:63:5b:4b:54:5a brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
        inet 192.168.25.15/24 brd 192.168.25.255 scope global eth0
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
        inet6 fe80::1e63:5b00:14b:545a/64 scope link 
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    root@control ~# podman stop test
    test


UID mapping with sysctl. Doesn't even run!

    root@control ~ [127]# podman run -d --name test --rm --network=podnettest --uidmap 0:1000:1 --uidmap 1:10000:65536 --sysctl net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf=1 quay.io/libpod/banner
    Error: crun: open `/proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/eth0/autoconf`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found



But autoconf _can_ be made to work:

    root@control ~ [127]# podman run -d --name test --rm --network=podnettest --uidmap 0:1000:1 --uidmap 1:10000:65536 quay.io/libpod/banner
    3dbe0286fa51b2eb6b6ced28409ed6dd98253b0090c0733bd2ec77580f8f17b5
    root@control ~# nsenter -t $(podman inspect --format '{{.State.Pid}}' test) -n sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf=1
    net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf = 1

Either:

    root@control ~# nsenter -t $(podman inspect --format '{{.State.Pid}}' test) -n ip link set eth0 down
    root@control ~# nsenter -t $(podman inspect --format '{{.State.Pid}}' test) -n ip link set eth0 up

Or:

    # Just wait ages for the next route advertisment to arrive

Then:

    root@control ~# podman exec -it test ip address
    1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
        link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
        inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
        inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    2: eth0@if3: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
        link/ether 1e:63:5b:4b:54:5a brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
        inet 192.168.25.15/24 brd 192.168.25.255 scope global eth0
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
        inet6 [--REDACTED IPv6 ADDRESS--]/64 scope global tentative dynamic flags 100 
           valid_lft 318126sec preferred_lft 318126sec
        inet6 fe80::1e63:5b00:14b:545a/64 scope link 
           valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    root@control ~# podman stop test
    test

comments.body:
That is because the sysctl doesn't exit at the time the container is created in the oci runtime. When using a userns the netns must be created the by the oci runtime and podman can only configure it afterwards so the order of network setup and oci runtime container creation is inverted depending on if a userns is used for the container.

There is no way to avoid this as I wrote in the netavark issue already
